누가복음 23:1.content: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.

누가복음 23:2.content: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”

누가복음 23:3.content: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “So you say.”

누가복음 23:4.content: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”

누가복음 23:5.content: But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”

누가복음 23:6.content: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

누가복음 23:7.content: When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.

누가복음 23:8.content: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.

누가복음 23:9.content: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

누가복음 23:10.content: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

누가복음 23:11.content: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.

누가복음 23:12.content: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.

누가복음 23:13.content: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,

누가복음 23:14.content: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and behold, having examined him before you, I found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.

누가복음 23:15.content: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.

누가복음 23:16.content: I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

누가복음 23:17.content: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.

누가복음 23:18.content: But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!”—

누가복음 23:19.content: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

누가복음 23:20.content: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,

누가복음 23:21.content: but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”

누가복음 23:22.content: He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

누가복음 23:23.content: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.

누가복음 23:24.content: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.

누가복음 23:25.content: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.

누가복음 23:26.content: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.

누가복음 23:27.content: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.

누가복음 23:28.content: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

누가복음 23:29.content: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’

누가복음 23:30.content: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’

누가복음 23:31.content: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”

누가복음 23:32.content: There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.

누가복음 23:33.content: When they came to the place that is called “The Skull”, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.

누가복음 23:34.content: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

누가복음 23:35.content: The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”

누가복음 23:36.content: The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,

누가복음 23:37.content: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”

누가복음 23:38.content: An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

누가복음 23:39.content: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”

누가복음 23:40.content: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Don’t you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?

누가복음 23:41.content: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”

누가복음 23:42.content: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”

누가복음 23:43.content: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”

누가복음 23:44.content: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.

누가복음 23:45.content: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.

누가복음 23:46.content: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.

누가복음 23:47.content: When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous man.”

누가복음 23:48.content: All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their breasts.

누가복음 23:49.content: All his acquaintances and the women who followed with him from Galilee stood at a distance, watching these things.

누가복음 23:50.content: Behold, a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man

누가복음 23:51.content: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom:

누가복음 23:52.content: this man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

누가복음 23:53.content: He took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.

누가복음 23:54.content: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

누가복음 23:55.content: The women, who had come with him out of Galilee, followed after, and saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.

누가복음 23:56.content: They returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
